top_signals.title: Latvia and Ukraine to open drone factory right on Baltic nation’s border with Russia
top_signals.summary: The Latgale region, where the new drone factory is to be built, is historically one of Latvia’s most economically disadvantaged.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Geopolitical escalation affecting commodities, shipping and cross-asset volatility.

top_signals.title: Brazilian Real Becomes ‘Collateral Damage’ as Dollar Roars Back
top_signals.summary: The Brazilian real is heading for its worst month this year as a rebound in the dollar and shifting interest-rate expectations push investors to unwind one of their most favored carry trades.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Central-bank repricing affecting yields, FX, liquidity and equities.

top_signals.title: The World Cup’s Ticket Pricing Gamble Paid Off
top_signals.summary: FIFA’s dynamic pricing strategy has angered fans but it’s helping generate record revenues, explains Bloomberg Opinion’s Juan Pablo Spinetto. (Source: Bloomberg)
top_signals.why_it_matters: AI and semiconductor developments impacting technology leadership and capex cycles.

top_signals.title: Visa, Stripe, Coinbase and more join Open USD stablecoin that shares reserve revenue
top_signals.summary: Expected to launch later this year, Open Standard said businesses will be able to mint and redeem Open USD without fees or volume limits.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Digital asset volatility and institutional crypto positioning changes.

top_signals.title: French biotech stock soars 40% after new data on experimental medicine
top_signals.summary: Shares of French biotech company Abivax soared Tuesday after it released fresh data on its lead drug for bowel disease.

top_signals.title: Lloyds accused of debanking left-wing media outlet The Canary
top_signals.summary: Lloyds Banking Group has been accused of debanking the left-wing news website The Canary in a move that has left the media outlet with “barely any funds”. The bank was slammed by the media firm on Tuesday for allegedly “withholding a substantial amount of our money” and debanking them after nearly a decade of use. [...]
